metadata.title: Information-Fidelity Coupling in Networks Is Linear, Not Quadratic: A Pre-Registered Cross-Domain Test, with Enforcement Latency as a Collapse Predictor (LISM)
metadata.description: The Linear Institution Stability Model (LISM). We test whether systemic viability couples linearly (E = U·D) or quadratically (E = U·D²) to two-hop communication fidelity, in the two domains admitting a valid, channel-intact test: a Saccharomyces cerevisiae protein interactome (N = 4,825; VIF = 1.003) and a pre-registered GitHub repository cohort (N = 992; quadratic disconfirmed, ΔAIC = -3.48). The linear model is favored in both; a previously reported 'anti-predictive' quadratic AUC (0.41) is shown to be a non-converged-fit artifact and corrected. Separately and pre-registered, enforcement latency τ_v is a robust leading indicator of failure (failed 50.6 d vs surviving 19.8 d; Mann-Whitney p ≈ 1e-31). Legislation and judicial coupling were run on live Congress.gov data and returned inconclusive for lack of an independent second hop. Includes full reproduction code, a domain-agnostic τ_v early-warning monitor, and an independent referee report.

creators.name: Mago, Labib
creators.affiliation: Novora Research Initiative, Open Science Division

metadata.notes: Raw public inputs (STRING v12, DEG, BioGRID) are not redistributed; fetch from source per REPRODUCIBILITY.md. Code: MIT; manuscript and documentation: CC-BY-4.0.
